full_text_rationale: Full text describes AiScientist maintaining durable workspace state through File-as-Bus artifacts so engineering agents can resume from persisted analyses, plans, code, and experimental evidence across long-horizon ML engineering loops, with PaperBench and MLE-Bench Lite evaluation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 013; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Guoxin Chen, Jie Chen, Lei Chen, Jiale Zhao, Fanzhe Meng, Wayne Xin Zhao, Ruihua Song, Cheng Chen, Ji-Rong Wen, Kai Jia
titulo: Toward Autonomous Long-Horizon Engineering for ML Research
formato_armazenamento: Permission-scoped File-as-Bus workspace containing paper_analysis artifacts, submission artifacts, prioritized_task.md, plan.md, implementation logs, experiment logs, code files, run outputs, and concise summaries that agents can repeatedly re-ground on.
framework_agente: AiScientist hierarchical ML-research engineering system with Orchestrator, Paper Analyzer, Planning Specialist, Implementation Specialist, Experiment Specialist, and shared File-as-Bus workspace
llm_utilizado: Gemini-3-Flash and GLM-5 in main evaluations; GPT-5.4 grader for PaperBench scoring
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; long-horizon task evaluation; ablation; cost comparison
metricas_desempenho: PaperBench score, MLE-Bench Lite any-medal rate, cost per task, ablation score drops
metricas_cl: Durable artifact-mediated continuity through File-as-Bus workspace, re-grounding of specialized agents, workspace-map summarization, and preserved implementation/experiment evidence.
baseline_comparado: BasicAgent, IterAgent, AiScientist without File-as-Bus, and MLE-Bench Lite leaderboard/baseline systems
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Workspace accumulates analyses, plans, code, logs, and experimental artifacts across each long-horizon task; no aggregate memory-size curve is reported.
custo_por_tarefa: PaperBench cost/task is reported: Gemini AiScientist $15.67 versus IterAgent $27.44; GLM-5 AiScientist $12.20 versus IterAgent $54.90. Full 20-task PaperBench grading costs about $832.
resultado_principal: AiScientist improves PaperBench scores from 20.60 to 30.52 with Gemini and from 22.37 to 33.73 with GLM-5. On MLE-Bench Lite, AiScientist reports 81.82% any-medal rate with both Gemini-3-Flash and GLM-5.
desempenho_vs_baseline: On PaperBench, AiScientist improves over IterAgent by 9.92 points with Gemini and 11.36 points with GLM-5. Removing File-as-Bus drops PaperBench by 6.41 points and MLE-Bench Lite any-medal rate by 31.82 points.
custo_vs_baseline: AiScientist is cheaper than IterAgent in the reported PaperBench setup: $15.67 versus $27.44 per task with Gemini and $12.20 versus $54.90 with GLM-5.
limitacoes: Evaluation is expensive and constrained to benchmarked ML research engineering tasks; performance depends on specialized agent roles, workspace conventions, and available compute/time budgets.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark selection, 24-hour task limit, H20 GPU environment, grading model, selected backbones, workspace permissions, and comparison to simplified agents may affect validity.
